artifacts.title: Forge Rescue Suite
artifacts.summary: Boundary-event rescue packets with replay validation, MachLib witness routing, and a restricted log-domain semantic theorem.
artifacts.claimBoundary: Approved for existing public/dev surfaces; no full optimizer semantic rewrite or hardware observation is claimed.
reviewHighlights: Replay-valid boundary event suite generated by Forge.
MachLib witnesses cover all four v0 rescue lanes.
log_domain_lift is promoted to restricted semantic rewrite; other lanes remain concrete sample invariants.
nonClaims: No unrestricted optimizer semantic rewrite theorem.
No certified safety or production-controller claim.

timeline.label: Boundary event captured
timeline.detail: Forge emits replayable rescue packets for named optimizer boundary events.

timeline.label: Replay checked
timeline.detail: Replay JSON validates and generated Explorer fixture stays aligned with the manifest.

timeline.label: Obligation registry reviewed
timeline.detail: Each rescue operator is routed to a semantic tier and MachLib witness path.

timeline.label: Semantic boundary set
timeline.detail: Only log_domain_lift has restricted semantic rewrite status in v0.

semanticReview.reviewer_note: Log-domain lift has a restricted semantic rewrite theorem; the other v0 lanes restore concrete local invariants. Full semantic rewrite correctness remains outside the v0 claim boundary.

artifacts.title: Electronics Trainer Packet
artifacts.summary: Simulated Trainer Board packet using the shared evidence grammar for trace, replay, validation, and claim flags.
artifacts.claimBoundary: Approved as simulated evidence only; hardware truth requires a real capture packet from the laptop-agent lane.
reviewHighlights: Packet follows the shared evidence grammar.
Simulation is explicit in the capture mode.
Future physical packets must carry live capture evidence before hardware truth claims.

timeline.label: Simulated trace packet
timeline.detail: Trainer Board packet is grammar-valid and replay-valid as simulated evidence.

timeline.label: Hardware boundary
timeline.detail: A real capture packet is required before hardware truth may be claimed.

semanticReview.reviewer_note: Grammar-valid packet; hardware truth requires a real capture packet.

artifacts.title: Monogate OS Replay Chain
artifacts.summary: Replay-native runtime evidence with identity CI, positive/negative fixture gates, and Observatory status; displayed as candidate evidence only.
artifacts.claimBoundary: Candidate only; not a bootable OS claim, not certified safety, and not production runtime assurance.
reviewHighlights: M7D identity CI reports pass with 24 replay frames across 5 kernels.
Positive identity gate and negative fixture gate both pass.
Unexpected negative-fixture pass count is zero.
nonClaims: No certified safety or production-controller claim.
No hardware action or live serial capture.

timeline.label: Replay frame chain
timeline.detail: M4C-M4F produced lifecycle, packet, comparator, and inspector evidence.

timeline.label: Identity gate
timeline.detail: M7B positive replay identity gate passed.

timeline.label: Negative fixture gate
timeline.detail: M7C negative fixtures did not unexpectedly pass.

timeline.label: Local CI entrypoint
timeline.detail: M7D combines positive and negative gates behind a strict local CI command.

semanticReview.reviewer_note: Replay-native runtime evidence remains candidate-only, not a bootable OS claim.

artifacts.title: Agent Output Evidence Demo
artifacts.summary: A bounded agent answer wrapped in validation, replay, claim flags, non-claims, and reviewer decision metadata.
artifacts.claimBoundary: Candidate only; demonstrates claim-governed agent output, not general agent truthfulness or autonomous deployment readiness.
reviewHighlights: The agent output is tied to explicit Monogate source paths.
Validation checks required fields, claim boundary, non-claims, and unsafe claim flags.
Replay records the contract, schema, and review-gate sources used by the output.
nonClaims: No certified safety or production-controller claim.

timeline.label: Task bounded
timeline.detail: The agent task asks for a summary and claim boundary from local Monogate evidence docs.

timeline.label: Output generated
timeline.detail: The output is accepted only as a reviewable artifact, not as an unqualified truth claim.

timeline.label: Validation applied
timeline.detail: The packet records required fields, non-claims, and unsafe claim flag checks.

timeline.label: Reviewer decision
timeline.detail: The artifact is visible as a research packet while broader validator coverage is still being built.

packetExtras.agentTask: Summarize the current Evidence Packet v0 contract and identify the claim boundary for a public artifact.
packetExtras.agentOutput: Evidence Packet v0 requires validation status, replay status, semantic strength, claim flags, claim boundary, non-claims, source paths, and reviewer decision. A public artifact may be surfaced only when the reviewer gate approves it and its claims remain bounded by the packet.

semanticReview.reviewer_note: This packet demonstrates claim-governed agent output. It validates that the output is bounded by cited Monogate artifacts; it does not prove general agent truthfulness.

artifacts.reviewNotes: Agent output is inspectable and replay-bounded, but remains candidate-only until broader packet-validator coverage exists.

artifacts.title: Monogate OS EML Bridge
artifacts.summary: Candidate EML/Forge-shaped OS bridge fixture set: log-domain lift and guard-clamp kernels run through mirrored no_std adapters with QEMU replay and sensitivity evidence.
artifacts.claimBoundary: Candidate only; these are fixture adapters, not Forge compiler outputs, not a Forge OS target, and not a bootable OS claim.
reviewHighlights: Bridge registry contains two independent EML-shaped kernels.
Negative fixtures block Forge-output overclaim, missing digests, missing trace kernels, and early public_ready.
M4C lifecycle and M5A sensitivity traces include the bridge kernels.
Bridge-family review validates each kernel independently and records proof-obligation stubs.
nonClaims: No formal equivalence proof between Forge output and guest adapters.
No certified safety or production-controller claim.

timeline.label: Bridge registry
timeline.detail: M8B registry records spec and adapter digests for both bridge kernels.

timeline.label: Second kernel
timeline.detail: M8C adds guard_clamp_v0 as an independent EML-shaped fixture adapter.

timeline.label: Negative fixtures
timeline.detail: Unsafe registry examples fail as expected.

timeline.label: Bridge family
timeline.detail: M8D validates each bridge kernel as an independently reviewable family member.

timeline.label: Diff and obligations
timeline.detail: M8E records source-to-adapter boundaries; M8F records proof-obligation cards.

artifacts.semanticsLabel: eml shaped fixture adapter qemu replay sensitivity

kernel_details.nonClaim: No full exp correctness proof.; No Forge compiler output claim.; No bootable OS or certified-safety claim.
No certified controller safety claim.; No production-controller claim.; No Forge compiler output claim.

semanticReview.reviewer_note: This proves the bridge discipline only: EML-shaped specs to mirrored no_std adapters to QEMU replay/sensitivity evidence.

artifacts.reviewNotes: EML/Forge-shaped OS bridge fixture remains candidate-only.
